Description: Vehicle stop conducted by four masked ICE agents in three unmarked vehicles. Three people on their way to a medical appointment were kidnapped. According to local journalists, all three had pending immigration statuses, with Arizona IDs and a work permit. The kidnappings took place in full view of a nearby school playground, prompting the school to cancel recess

4-5 ICE agents in at least three unmarked vehicles followed a married couple to the parking lot of an Arizona government office. According to a sworn affidavit given by one of the people targeted, when the vehicles cornered the couple as they walked toward the office, they thought they were being robbed and ran. One of the ICE agents used a vehicle to ram the man and knock him to the ground. They then proceeded to “beat him severely with their fists”. Eventually he was handcuffed and stuffed into one of the vehicles. According to the wife’s affidavit, “As they cuffed my husband after beating him, one female agent held me by the arms, and I asked her not to touch me. She replied with a warning: “be careful what you do otherwise my son [name redacted] may not have anyone to pick him up from school.” I remember these words very well because this threat terrified me, especially since they knew my son’s name.” The kidnapped man was taken to an ICE detention facility and his wife was left in the parking lot with their vehicle.

Description: Mysterious incident involving two uniformed Border Patrol agents in marked vehicles and another in plain clothes in an unmarked pickup truck with police lights. A chain was attached to the unmarked truck and it was used to tow an SUV with Sonoran plates, which the Border Patrol agents described as a “stranded motorist”. But Border Patrol agents are not permitted to tow civilian vehicles with chains, so we think either this was also an unmarked Border Patrol vehicle or it was the “ghost car” of someone who had already been kidnapped.
